secondary_archetype: Multi-level consumer/marketplace PM across Merchant, Consumer, Ops, Ads, Logistics, New Verticals

level_signals: 5+ years of Product Management Industry Experience
Role title explicitly states Multiple Levels

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 5+ years of Product Management Industry Experience
You have 4+ years of user-facing experience in industries such as eCommerce, technology or multi-sided marketplaces
You have proven abilities in driving product strategy, vision, and roadmap alignment
You have experience presenting business reviews to senior executives
You have empathy for the users you build for
You are passionate about DoorDash and the problems we are solving for

scope.surface: One of: Merchant, Consumer, Operational Excellence, Ads, Logistics, or New Verticals pillars
scope.business_model: B2C and B2B marketplace, on-demand local delivery
core_responsibilities: Drive the product definition, strategy, and long term vision. You own the roadmap
Work closely with cross-functional teams of designers, operators, data scientists and engineers
Communicate product plans, benefits and results to key stakeholders including leadership team

green_flags: Compensation bands explicitly stated across three levels (I4, I5, I6)
Broad pillar options give candidates visibility into multiple product domains
Strong benefits package including 16 weeks paid parental leave, equity, 401(k) matching
Multiple hiring levels posted together, suggesting active hiring pipeline
red_flags: JD does not specify which pillar(s) are actively hiring, making it difficult to assess actual scope
No specifics on level placement process — candidates may not know which level they are being evaluated for
Compensation range spans $130K–$285K, an extremely wide band reflecting three levels, which may obscure actual offer expectations
No mention of visa sponsorship, leaving sponsorship availability unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp bands I4–I6 and '5+ years' requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and DoorDash's known model)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd set to outer bounds of all three level ranges combined; individual ranges are I4: $130,600–$192,000, I5: $159,800–$235,000, I6: $193,800–$285,000
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'hybrid of remote work and in-person collaboration' and mention of remote jobs associated with NYC office)
ambiguities: JD mentions 'remote work' as part of hybrid but also references 'remote jobs associated with office in NYC' in legal notice — unclear if fully remote candidates in non-NYC markets are eligible or if role requires proximity to an office
No cities explicitly named for this role beyond the NYC legal notice; cities_listed left empty accordingly
Compensation ranges represent three distinct levels (I4, I5, I6); it is unclear which level a given candidate will be slotted into without recruiter discussion
